seoTitle: Set Correct Answers and Points in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Configure automated grading in Microsoft Forms by flagging correct choices, entering text answer keys, and assigning point values.
intro: The most common mistake people make when trying to set up automated grading in Microsoft Forms is starting from a standard Form instead of a Quiz, since only quizzes support answer keys and points. This guide walks through building a graded question correctly from the start.

steps.heading: Start with New Quiz, not New Form
steps.body: From the home directory, make sure you select the New Quiz option rather than the standard New Form tile.

steps.heading: Add your question
steps.body: Click Add New and choose your preferred question type, then write your query and fill in the response options.

steps.heading: Flag the correct choice
steps.body: Hover over the correct response row and click the green checkmark icon labeled Correct Answer to lock it into the key.

steps.heading: Set text-based answer keys
steps.body: For short answer questions, tap Add Answer beneath the text box and type in valid word variations that should count as correct.

steps.heading: Assign points and test
steps.body: Enter a numerical value in the Points field, toggle Required, then use Preview to submit a test response and confirm the grading works.

faq.question: Why don't I see an option to mark a correct answer?
faq.answer: This usually means you started with a standard Form instead of a Quiz — only the Quiz format supports answer keys.

faq.question: Can text answers be graded automatically too?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add multiple valid text variations under Add Answer so short text responses are auto-graded.

faq.question: How do I control how much a question is worth?
faq.answer: The Points input field at the lower border of the question lets you assign a specific numerical value.

faq.question: Can I test grading before publishing the quiz?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Preview button to submit trial responses and confirm point triggers work as expected.
